instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use `*XX` (asterisk + uppercase hex) encoding for CloudWatch fragment values. NEVER use standard `%XX` percent-encoding.
instructions.relevant_when: Constructing any CloudWatch deep-link URL that contains encoded characters in the fragment.
instructions.why_given: CloudWatch uses *XX encoding, not %XX. Using %XX produces a silently broken URL that loads the wrong page or a blank view.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS supply epoch timestamps in seconds (10-digit) for Logs Insights URLs. NEVER pass milliseconds.
instructions.relevant_when: Building a Logs Insights deep-link URL with a start/end time range.
instructions.why_given: Milliseconds produce a time range in the year 57,000+, returning zero results with no error message.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS include `region=` in the query string as well as the subdomain when constructing any CloudWatch URL.
instructions.why_given: Omitting the region= query parameter causes the console to load data for the browser's last-used region, not the intended one.

instructions.instruction: When investigating a triggered alarm, ALWAYS navigate to the alarm detail page first, not the Metrics graph.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks to investigate or verify a CloudWatch alarm.
instructions.why_given: The alarm detail page shows exactly which metric drove the alarm — it may be a Logs metric filter, not the native AWS/Lambda Errors metric.

instructions.instruction: Leave structural characters (`~`, `(`, `)`, `'`) literal in CloudWatch fragment URLs. Do NOT apply *XX encoding to them.
instructions.relevant_when: Encoding any CloudWatch URL fragment value.
instructions.why_given: These characters are structural delimiters in the CloudWatch SPA URL format and must remain unencoded for the URL to parse correctly.
